title: Pollo alla Cacciatora Hunter Style Chicken

seo.title: Easy Chicken Cacciatore Recipe (Pollo alla Cacciatora)
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Italian Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora) with our easy recipe. Tender chicken, rich tomato sauce, and fresh herbs. Perfect for a hearty family meal!
seo.h1: Authentic Italian Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora)
h2Headings: What is Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora)?
Ingredients You'll Need for Hunter Style Chicken
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Chicken Cacciatore

seo.imageAlt: A rustic plate of authentic Italian Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora) with tender chicken, bell peppers, and olives.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a taste of Italy with this hearty and flavorful Hunter Style Chicken, perfect for any family dinner.

description: Pollo alla Cacciatora, or Hunter Style Chicken, is a beloved Italian classic featuring tender chicken braised in a rich, savory tomato sauce with bell peppers, onions, and aromatic herbs. This rustic and hearty dish brings the flavors of the Italian countryside straight to your table, perfect for a comforting family meal. Its robust profile and simple preparation make it a timeless favorite that truly embodies Italian home cooking.

ingredients.notes: thighs, drumsticks, or a whole cut-up chicken, skin on and bone-in
red and green, cored and sliced

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken and Season
instructions.text: Pat the chicken pieces dry with paper towels. Season generously with salt and black pepper on all sides.

instructions.title: Brown the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the chicken pieces in batches, skin-side down, and brown deeply on all sides, about 5-7 minutes per batch. Remove the browned chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics and Vegetables
instructions.text: In the same pot, add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the sliced bell peppers and mushrooms, cooking for 5-7 minutes until they begin to soften.

instructions.title: Deglaze and Add Tomatoes
instructions.text: Pour in the white wine, scraping any browned bits from the bottom of the pot. Let it simmer for 2-3 minutes until the alcohol evaporates. Stir in the tomato paste, crushed tomatoes, chicken broth, rosemary, thyme, and bay leaf. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer.

instructions.title: Braise the Chicken
instructions.text: Return the browned chicken pieces to the pot, nestling them into the sauce. Ensure the chicken is mostly submerged. Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for at least 30 minutes, or until the chicken is tender and cooked through.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the pitted Kalamata olives and continue to simmer uncovered for another 5-10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Remove the bay leaf before serving. Garnish generously with fresh chopped parsley and serve hot with polenta, crusty bread, or pasta.

tips: For extra depth of flavor, marinate the chicken in a little olive oil, salt, pepper, and dried herbs for at least 30 minutes before browning.
If you prefer a thicker sauce, you can remove the chicken at the end and reduce the sauce further over medium heat until it reaches your desired consistency.
Chicken Cacciatore tastes even better the next day, as the flavors have more time to meld. It stores well in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days and can also be frozen for longer storage.
article:
Pollo alla Cacciatora: The Ultimate Guide to Hunter Style Italian Chicken

Imagine a dish that tells a story of rustic Italian kitchens, hearty ingredients, and simmering flavors that fill your home with an irresistible aroma. That dish is Pollo alla Cacciatora, or Hunter Style Chicken, a beloved classic that embodies the very essence of Italian comfort food. More than just a meal, it's an experience – a rich, savory stew of tender chicken, vibrant tomatoes, sweet bell peppers, and fragrant herbs, all slow-cooked to perfection. Whether you’re a seasoned home cook or new to Italian cuisine, mastering this dish will bring a taste of Italy right to your dining table.

## What is Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora)?

The name "Cacciatora" literally translates to "hunter style," hinting at the dish's origins. Historically, hunters would prepare this meal with whatever game they caught, often chicken or rabbit, and whatever vegetables and herbs they could forage in the woods. This rustic approach means there isn't one single, immutable recipe; rather, it’s a style of cooking that celebrates fresh, seasonal ingredients and simple, robust flavors.

At its heart, Pollo alla Cacciatora features chicken pieces (often bone-in, skin-on for maximum flavor) braised in a savory sauce. The base typically includes onions, garlic, and bell peppers (red, green, or yellow), sautéed until softened. Diced or crushed tomatoes form the liquid foundation, often complemented by a splash of red or white wine, which adds depth and acidity. Herbs like rosemary, thyme, and oregano are crucial for that authentic Italian fragrance. Some variations might include mushrooms or olives, but the core elements remain consistent: tender chicken, a rich tomato-based sauce, and aromatic vegetables, all coming together in a symphony of flavor that makes Chicken Cacciatore a timeless favorite. It's a dish that's both elegant enough for a dinner party and comforting enough for a cozy family meal.

## Ingredients You'll Need for Hunter Style Chicken

Creating an authentic Pollo alla Cacciatora starts with selecting quality ingredients. Here's what you'll typically need to gather:

*   **Chicken:** 3-4 lbs bone-in, skin-on chicken pieces (thighs, drumsticks, or a mix). The bone and skin add immense flavor and moisture during the long simmer.
*   **Olive Oil:** 2-3 tablespoons, for browning the chicken and sautéing vegetables.
*   **Onion:** 1 large yellow onion, chopped.
*   **Garlic:** 4-6 cloves, minced.
*   **Bell Peppers:** 2 large bell peppers (e.g., one red, one green), deseeded and sliced into strips.
*   **Mushrooms (Optional):** 8 oz cremini or button mushrooms, sliced.
*   **Tomatoes:** 1 (28-ounce) can crushed tomatoes or diced tomatoes. High-quality San Marzano tomatoes are recommended for their superior flavor.
*   **Wine:** 1/2 cup dry red or white wine (e.g., Chianti, Pinot Grigio). Alternatively, chicken broth can be used.
*   **Chicken Broth:** 1/2 cup (if not using wine, increase to 1 cup).
*   **Herbs:** 1 tablespoon fresh rosemary, chopped; 1 teaspoon dried oregano; 1 teaspoon dried thyme. Fresh parsley for garnish.
*   **Seasoning:** Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste.
*   **Optional Add-ins:** 1/2 cup Kalamata olives, pitted; a pinch of red pepper flakes for a touch of heat.

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Chicken Cacciatore

Making Pollo alla Cacciatora is a labor of love, but the process is straightforward and incredibly rewarding.

1.  **Prepare and Season the Chicken:** Pat the chicken pieces dry with paper towels. Season generously with salt and black pepper on all sides.
2.  **Brown the Chicken:** Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven or heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high heat. Working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding, add the chicken skin-side down and brown for 5-7 minutes per side until golden and crispy. Remove the chicken from the pot and set aside.
3.  **Sauté the Aromatics:** Reduce the heat to medium. Add the chopped onion and bell peppers to the pot, scraping up any browned bits from the chicken. Sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened. Add the minced garlic and mushrooms (if using), and cook for another 2-3 minutes until fragrant.
4.  **Deglaze with Wine:** Pour in the wine (or chicken broth) and scrape the bottom of the pot to release any remaining flavorful browned bits. Let it simmer for 2-3 minutes until the liquid has slightly reduced.
5.  **Add Tomatoes and Herbs:** Stir in the crushed tomatoes, chicken broth (if using in addition to wine), rosemary, oregano, and thyme. Season with a pinch more salt and pepper, and red pepper flakes if desired.
6.  **Simmer the Chicken:** Return the browned chicken pieces to the pot, nestling them into the sauce. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and cook for 45-60 minutes, or until the chicken is fork-tender and cooked through.
7.  **Finish and Serve:** If the sauce is too thin, remove the chicken and simmer the sauce uncovered for a few minutes to reduce. Stir in pitted olives if using. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed. Garnish with fresh parsley before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Pollo alla Cacciatora

*   **Don't Skip Browning:** Browning the chicken creates a rich, complex flavor base (Maillard reaction) that is essential for a deeply flavored sauce.
*   **Quality Matters:** Use the best quality canned tomatoes you can find, ideally San Marzano. Their natural sweetness and acidity make a significant difference.
*   **Bone-In, Skin-On:** While boneless, skinless chicken can be used, bone-in, skin-on pieces yield a much more flavorful and moist result. The skin also crisps up beautifully during browning.
*   **Low and Slow:** Patience is key. A long, slow simmer allows the flavors to meld beautifully and ensures the chicken becomes incredibly tender.
*   **Resting Time:** Like many stews, Chicken Cacciatore benefits from a short resting period (5-10 minutes) off the heat before serving. This allows the flavors to settle and the chicken to reabsorb its juices.
*   **Make Ahead:** This dish often tastes even better the next day, as the flavors have more time to deepen. It reheats wonderfully.

## Serving Suggestions for Italian Hunter Style Chicken

Pollo alla Cacciatora is a complete meal in itself, but it truly shines when paired with simple accompaniments that allow its rich flavors to take center stage.

*   **Pasta:** Serve it over a bed of al dente pasta like tagliatelle, pappardelle, or even spaghetti. The pasta soaks up the delicious sauce beautifully.
*   **Crusty Bread:** A must-have! A warm, crusty baguette or Italian bread is perfect for mopping up every last drop of the savory sauce.
*   **Polenta:** Creamy polenta provides a comforting and authentic Italian pairing, offering a smooth counterpoint to the robust chicken.
*   **Roasted Potatoes:** Simple roasted potatoes or a side of mashed potatoes can also be a wonderful addition.
*   **Simple Green Salad:** A light, refreshing green salad with a vinaigrette dressing helps to cut through the richness of the dish.

## Variations and Substitutions for Chicken Cacciatore

While the classic recipe is fantastic, Pollo alla Cacciatora is incredibly versatile and allows for creative modifications based on your preferences or what you have on hand.

*   **Chicken Cuts:** If you prefer, you can use boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, though cooking time will be reduced. Just be careful not to overcook them.
*   **Vegetable Additions:** Feel free to add other vegetables like carrots, celery, zucchini, or even green beans during the simmering process.
*   **Spice Level:** For a spicier version, increase the amount of red pepper flakes or add a finely chopped fresh chili pepper.
*   **Wine Alternatives:** If you prefer not to cook with wine, simply substitute it with an equal amount of good quality chicken broth.
*   **Herb Variations:** Experiment with different fresh herbs. Marjoram or fresh basil added at the end can also provide lovely aromatic notes.
*   **Olives and Capers:** For a briny, tangy kick, stir in a handful of pitted Kalamata or Castelvetrano olives and a tablespoon of capers during the last 10 minutes of cooking.

Pollo alla Cacciatora is more than just a recipe; it's a celebration of simple ingredients transformed into something extraordinary through patient cooking. Its hearty, comforting nature makes it a perfect dish for any season, bringing warmth and flavor to your table. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to enjoy a truly authentic taste of Italy.
